summary: Private market data, pricing, trading, and API infrastructure.
editor_note: Private markets are still priced through relationships and stale spreadsheets; Caplight turns that into a data product. Secondary pricing, MarketPrice, company and investor databases, API/MCP access, and a $16M Series A make it more like market plumbing than another VC dashboard. The tension is whether users trade enough inside its network.

sources.label: PR Newswire Series A funding report

product_evidence.claim: The official site exposes Secondary Data and Trading, Caplight MarketPrice, Company Database, Investor Database, Sourcing, and API and MCP products.
The homepage describes historical pricing trends, real-time market alerts, and data sourced from primary and secondary transactions.
The Series A product note says Caplight sits on proprietary secondary-market data, funding-round data, platform conversations, and registered-broker-dealer pipes.

market_context.primary_user: Private-market investors, brokers, secondary buyers and sellers, and research teams that need pricing, data, and transaction workflows.
market_context.differentiation: Caplight combines secondary pricing data, company and investor databases, trading workflow, and API/MCP access in one private-market platform.
market_context.why_now: Private-company financing has become larger and more frequent, increasing demand for secondary pricing signals and transaction infrastructure.

risks.claim: The open question is whether enough private-market transaction flow happens on Caplight to make the data advantage durable against incumbents and bank-led platforms.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on the official product surface and funding-source review.
